narration: Word Search tries one path through the board while matching the word one letter at a time.
callout: The next move can go up, down, left, or right.

narration: When a cell is used in the current path, we mark it so the same path cannot reuse it.
decision: Mark now, explore neighbors, then restore the cell afterward.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Why do we mark a board cell while exploring a path?

choices.label: So the same path does not reuse that cell
choices.feedback: Correct. One search path cannot use the same cell twice.

choices.label: So the board becomes sorted
choices.feedback: Not this one. The mark is only temporary path memory.

narration: If a path fails, undo restores the cell so a different starting path can still use it later.
callout: Without restore, one failed path could wrongly block later paths.
